name: Cabeçó d'Or Viewpoint Loop from Sant Joan d'Alacant
name_original: El Mirador del Cabeço d'Or — Circular desde Sant Joan d'Alacant
description: This moderate 33.1-mile road cycling route from Sant Joan d'Alacant offers panoramic views from the Mirador del Cabeço d'Or.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Sant Joan d'Alacant offer diverse terrain, from coastal stretches to mountainous ascents. The region features well-maintained routes with varying elevation gains, catering to different fitness levels. Cyclists can explore natural landscapes, including reservoirs and viewpoints overlooking the Costa Blanca. The area is characterized by a mix of coastal views and inland hills, providing a varied cycling experience. Best road cycling routes around Sant Joan D'Alacant The most popular road cycling route is Cabeçó d'Or Viewpoint Loop from Sant Joan d'Alacant, a 33.1 miles (53.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Avenida de Niza – Amadorio Reservoir loop from Instituto, a difficult 44.7 miles (72.0 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and scenic views of the Amadorio Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Alto de Busot – View of the Costa Blanca loop from l'Entredós, a 33.1 miles (53.3 km) trail leading through the hills of Busot, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Joan D'Alacant is defined by coastal views, reservoir vistas, and varied mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Sant Joan D'Alacant are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. More than 16,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Joan D'Alacant's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Joan D'Alacant?
A: There are over 200 road cycling routes around Sant Joan D'Alacant, offering a wide array of options for various fitness levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of fitness level is required for road cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes around Sant Joan D'Alacant cater to a broad range of fitness levels. You'll find approximately 50 easy routes, nearly 100 moderate routes, and over 60 difficult routes. This diversity ensures options from relaxed rides with gentle ups and downs to challenging ascents with significant elevation gains, some exceeding 3,000 ft.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Sant Joan D'Alacant?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891970996/cabeco-d-or-viewpoint-loop-from-sant-joan-d-alacant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Viewpoint Loop from Sant Joan d'Alacant&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22170537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Busot – View of the Costa Blanca loop from l'Entredós&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I expect to see on road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Sant Joan D'Alacant offer panoramic views of the Costa Blanca and diverse landscapes. You can enjoy vistas from destinations like the Mirador del Cabeço d'Or and picturesque views of the Embalse del Amadorio (Amadorio Reservoir). Notable landmarks include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/607512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara Castle&lt;/a&gt; and natural beauty spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1040122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Illeta dels Banyets&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are easy road cycling routes perfect for families and beginners. The region offers routes with gentle ups and downs and smooth asphalt, ideal for a relaxed ride. These routes often feature well-maintained paths, making them accessible for those new to road cycling or looking for a leisurely experience.

Q: What is the terrain like for road cycling around Sant Joan D'Alacant?
A: The terrain for road cycling around Sant Joan D'Alacant is diverse, featuring a mix of coastal stretches and mountainous ascents. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces suitable for road bikes, with routes offering everything from flat sections along the coast to challenging climbs through inland hills like those around Busot.

Q: Are there challenging road bike climbs for advanced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For advanced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3973639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agost to Maigmó Road – Alto de Busot loop from Platja de Mutxavista&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, reaching over 1,300 meters. The area provides numerous opportunities for demanding ascents and long-distance rides.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Sant Joan D'Alacant?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 370 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from stunning coastal views to challenging mountain climbs, and the well-maintained routes that cater to all ability levels. The scenic beauty, especially around the Amadorio Reservoir and the Costa Blanca viewpoints, is frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I rent a road bike in Sant Joan D'Alacant?
A: While specific rental services are not detailed in the guide, Sant Joan D'Alacant and the broader Alicante province are popular cycling destinations. It is common to find bike rental shops in such areas, offering various types of bicycles, including road bikes. It is advisable to search locally for 'road bike hire Sant Joan D'Alacant' to find current options.

Q: Are there any natural parks or protected areas accessible by road bike?
A: While the routes themselves might not directly enter all natural parks, the wider Alicante province, where Sant Joan D'Alacant is located, is home to impressive natural parks such as Peñón de Ifach, Montgó Natural Park, and Font Roja Natural Park. Many road cycling routes offer views of or pass near these unique landscapes, providing opportunities to explore beyond cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Joan D'Alacant?
A: Sant Joan D'Alacant benefits from a Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. However, the region's mild winters can also be ideal for those seeking to escape colder climates.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of the coast?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the Sant Joan D'Alacant area provide stunning coastal views. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic vistas of the Costa Blanca and the bay of Alicante, often incorporating sections along the coastline or climbs that offer elevated perspectives of the sea.
tips:
The Cabeçó d'Or viewpoint offers you incredible views of the entire landscape that follows its slopes.

Not only this, but it also has fauna characteristic of the area. The presence of the mountain goat, the eagle owl, the peregrine falcon and a pair of golden eagles stands out.
Good road surface, little traffic on weekends, you can continue the route towards Relleu with a quite enjoyable road.

faqs.question: How long does this road cycling route typically take to complete?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 53.3 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 835 meters. It typically takes around 2 hours and 42 minutes to complete, though this can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered a moderate road cycling route and is best suited for riders with good fitness. While it features primarily well-paved surfaces, cyclists should anticipate challenging climbs with gradients that can range from 7% to 11%.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride. However, be prepared for challenging climbs with gradients that can reach between 7% and 11%. While some sections in the broader area may be unpaved, this specific road cycling route aims for paved surfaces.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning and diverse scenery throughout the ride. Key highlights include the Viewpoint of Cabeçó d'Or, which offers panoramic views of landmarks and cities like Calpe, Benidorm, Alicante, and Elx. You'll also pass through the Alto de Busot.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to road cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, generally the best times for road cycling in the Alicante province are spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, avoiding the intense summer heat and cooler winter months, making the challenging climbs more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Sant Joan d'Alacant. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you would typically look for public parking within Sant Joan d'Alacant to begin your ride. The inland roads of Alicante province are generally calm, providing a good environment for starting your journey.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, and while there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, it is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs due to the speed, distance, and challenging climbs involved. It's best enjoyed as a human-only cycling experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to road cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and access points.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, for those needing a break, cafes are available on the observation deck at the Viewpoint of Cabeçó d'Or. Additionally, as the route starts and ends in Sant Joan d'Alacant, you'll find various amenities there.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You may encounter parts of El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Cabeçó d'Or, Camino Viejo de Aigües, Camí del Cementeri, and Camí de Lampa.

faqs.question: What is the origin of the name 'Cabeçó d'Or'?
faqs.answer: Despite meaning 'Golden Head' in Valencian, the name 'Cabeçó d'Or' is thought to originate from an ancient Iberian word for water (UR) rather than actual gold. Historically, there were attempts to find precious metals there, but the name's true root is likely tied to water.
